id: how + adjective + subject + verb as noun clause
intention: 用 how + 形容词 + 主语 + 动词 表达程度，整体作名词从句跟在介词或动词后
meaning: A wh-clause using how + adjective/adverb + subject + verb to express degree; acts as a noun and can follow prepositions (for, at, about) or verbs (know, realize, imagine, understand)
usageContext:
Pattern: [preposition / verb] + how + adj/adv + subject + verb

After prepositions:
- I was not prepared for how difficult it would be.
- I was surprised at how quickly he learned.
- Nobody warned me about how stressful onboarding would be.

After verbs:
- I had no idea how hard it would be.
- You will not believe how long this took.
- I now understand how complex the system is.

Note: word order is statement order (S + V), NOT question order.
  Wrong: I realized how difficult was the task.
  Right:  I realized how difficult the task was.
anchorSentence: I was not mentally prepared for how difficult the code review would be.
reviewQuestions: how + adjective + S + V 从句里，语序是疑问句语序还是陈述句语序？
用 I had no idea how ... 造一个关于工作中超出预期的事的句子。
用 I was surprised at how ... 造一个真实的句子。
